---
title: "Context POS Data Processing Steps 02-08"
output: html_document
---

This organized R Markdown file migrates the Step 02-08 data-processing code in execution order. Replace every `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH` placeholder before running.

## Step 02: Save the 91 Whitelisted Raw Features

Read the cleaned homonym, polyseme, and monoseme tables; keep the metadata columns plus the 91 feature whitelist; write the raw feature-filtered files.

```{r step_02}
RAW_HOM  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/homonym_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"
RAW_POLY <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/polyseme_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"
RAW_MONO <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/monoseme_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"

OUT_DIR  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved"
dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

OUT_HOM  <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "homonym_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx")
OUT_MONO <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "monoseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx")
OUT_POLY <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "polyseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx")

# Processing step.
# install.packages(c("readxl", "openxlsx", "dplyr", "stringr"))
library(readxl)
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# Feature whitelist and feature-column handling.
FEATURES_KEEP <- c(
  "ValenceArousal_Valence_Mean","ValenceArousal_Valence_SD",
  "ValenceArousal_Arousal_Mean","ValenceArousal_Arousal_SD",
  "ValenceArousal_Men_Valence_Mean","ValenceArousal_Men_Valence_SD",
  "ValenceArousal_Women_Valence_Mean","ValenceArousal_Women_Valence_SD",
  "ValenceArousal_Men_Arousal_Mean","ValenceArousal_Men_Arousal_SD",
  "ValenceArousal_Women_Arousal_Mean","ValenceArousal_Women_Arousal_SD",
  "SUBTLEXWord_WCount","SUBTLEXWord_W/million","SUBTLEXWord_logW",
  "SUBTLEXWord_W-CD","SUBTLEXWord_W-CD%","SUBTLEXWord_logW-CD",
  "MELD_RT","MELD_RTSD","MELD_zRT","MELD_zRTSD","MELD_ERR",
  "Concreteness_Mean of Valid Ratings","Concreteness_SD of Valid Ratings",
  "CLD_Phonemes","CLD_Frequency","CLD_C1Frequency","CLD_C2Frequency",
  "CLD_FrequencyRaw","CLD_C1FrequencyRaw","CLD_C2FrequencyRaw",
  "CLD_FrequencyWeibo","CLD_C1FrequencyWeibo","CLD_C2FrequencyWeibo",
  "CLD_FrequencyRawWeibo","CLD_C1FrequencyRawWeibo","CLD_C2FrequencyRawWeibo",
  "CLD_PhonologicalFrequency","CLD_C1PhonologicalFrequency","CLD_C2PhonologicalFrequency",
  "CLD_C1FamilySize","CLD_C2FamilySize","CLD_C1FamilyFrequency","CLD_C2FamilyFrequency",
  "CLD_Strokes","CLD_C1Strokes","CLD_C2Strokes","CLD_C1Pixels","CLD_C2Pixels",
  "CLD_C1PictureSize","CLD_C2PictureSize","CLD_C1OLDPixels","CLD_C2OLDPixels",
  "CLD_PhonologicalN","CLD_C1PhonologicalN","CLD_C2PhonologicalN",
  "CLD_PLD","CLD_C1PLD","CLD_C2PLD",
  "CLD_MeanPhonemeFrequency","CLD_C1MeanPhonemeFrequency","CLD_C2MeanPhonemeFrequency",
  "CLD_MinPhonemeFrequency","CLD_C1MinPhonemeFrequency","CLD_C2MinPhonemeFrequency",
  "CLD_MaxPhonemeFrequency","CLD_C1MaxPhonemeFrequency","CLD_C2MaxPhonemeFrequency",
  "CLD_C1InitialPhonemeFrequency","CLD_C2InitialPhonemeFrequency",
  "CLD_MeanDiphoneFrequency","CLD_MinDiphoneFrequency","CLD_MaxDiphoneFrequency",
  "CLD_C1Friends","CLD_C2Friends","CLD_C1HomographTokens","CLD_C2HomographTokens",
  "CLD_C1FriendsFrequency","CLD_C2FriendsFrequency",
  "CLD_C1HomophonesFrequency","CLD_C2HomophonesFrequency",
  "CLD_C1Entropy","CLD_C1BackwardEntropy",
  "CLD_PMI","CLD_PSPMI","CLD_TScore","CLD_PSTScore",
  "CLD_C1ConditionalProbability","CLD_C1BackwardConditionalProbability",
  "CLD_EntropyCharacterFrequencies"
)

# Processing step.
# Processing step.
H_COL_INDEX <- 8

# Processing step.
filter_keep_front_and_whitelist_after_H <- function(path_in, path_out, features_keep, h_col_index) {
  message("Reading file: ", path_in)
  df <- read_xlsx(path_in)

  all_cols <- names(df)
  n_cols   <- length(all_cols)

  if (h_col_index > n_cols) {
    stop("H_COL_INDEX exceeds the number of columns. Please check whether it is really the H-column index.")
  }

  # Processing step.
  left_cols <- all_cols[1:(h_col_index-1)]

  # Processing step.
  right_candidate_cols <- all_cols[h_col_index:n_cols]

  # Processing step.
  norm_key <- function(x) gsub("\\s+", "", tolower(x))

  right_keep <- right_candidate_cols[
    norm_key(right_candidate_cols) %in% norm_key(features_keep)
  ]

  # Processing step.
  final_cols <- c(left_cols, right_keep)

  # Processing step.
  df_out <- df[, final_cols, drop = FALSE]

  # Processing step.
  message("total columns: ", n_cols,
          " | columns kept before H: ", length(left_cols),
          " | whitelist feature columns from H onward: ", length(right_keep),
          " | final output columns: ", length(final_cols))

  # Processing step.
  missing_in_this_file <- setdiff(
    features_keep,
    right_candidate_cols[ norm_key(right_candidate_cols) %in% norm_key(features_keep) ]
  )
  if (length(missing_in_this_file) > 0) {
    message("Warning: the following whitelist features were not found in this file or after the H-column boundary:")
    message(paste("  -", missing_in_this_file, collapse = "\n"))
  }

  message("Writing file: ", path_out)
  write.xlsx(df_out, path_out, overwrite = TRUE)

  invisible(df_out)
}

# Candidate-set preparation.
hom_filtered  <- filter_keep_front_and_whitelist_after_H(
  path_in      = RAW_HOM,
  path_out     = OUT_HOM,
  features_keep= FEATURES_KEEP,
  h_col_index  = H_COL_INDEX
)

mono_filtered <- filter_keep_front_and_whitelist_after_H(
  path_in      = RAW_MONO,
  path_out     = OUT_MONO,
  features_keep= FEATURES_KEEP,
  h_col_index  = H_COL_INDEX
)

poly_filtered <- filter_keep_front_and_whitelist_after_H(
  path_in      = RAW_POLY,
  path_out     = OUT_POLY,
  features_keep= FEATURES_KEEP,
  h_col_index  = H_COL_INDEX
)

message("Completed. Output directory: ", OUT_DIR)
```

Expected output:
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/homonym_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx`
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/polyseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx`
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/monoseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx`

## Step 03: Normalize the 91 Features by Headword

For each lexical group, deduplicate by headword, compute z-scores on the feature columns, and restore the normalized values to the original row shape.

```{r step_03}
FILE_HOM  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/homonym_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"
FILE_POLY <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/polyseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"
FILE_MONO <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/monoseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"

OUT_DIR   <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/03_normaliation_91"
dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)


# install.packages(c("readxl","openxlsx","dplyr","readr","stringr"))
library(readxl)
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)

H_COL_INDEX <- 8

# Processing step.

# Processing step.
read_any <- function(path){
  if (grepl("\\.xlsx?$", path, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    read_xlsx(path)
  } else if (grepl("\\.csv$", path, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    read_csv(path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  } else {
    stop("Only .xlsx or .csv files are supported: ", path)
  }
}

# Processing step.
to_numeric_soft <- function(v){
  if (is.numeric(v)) return(v)
  if (is.character(v)){
    v2 <- gsub("[,\\s]", "", v)
    return(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(v2)))
  }
  suppressWarnings(as.numeric(v))
}

# Z-score normalization.
zscore_vector <- function(x){
  mu  <- mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  sdv <- sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.na(sdv) || sdv == 0) {
    return(ifelse(is.na(x), NA_real_, 0))
  } else {
    return((x - mu) / sdv)
  }
}

# Processing step.
round_numeric_cols <- function(df, digits = 5){
  df %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, digits)))
}

# Processing step.
process_one_file <- function(in_path, out_dir, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX){

  message("Processing file: ", in_path)
  df_raw <- read_any(in_path)

  # Processing step.
  original_cols <- names(df_raw)

  # Processing step.
  col_head <- if ("headword" %in% names(df_raw)) {
    "headword"
  } else {
    nm <- names(df_raw)
    hit <- nm[trimws(tolower(nm)) == "headword"]
    if (length(hit) == 0) stop("Could not find the headword column: ", in_path)
    hit[1]
  }

  # Processing step.
  if (h_col_index > ncol(df_raw)) {
    stop("This file has fewer columns than H_COL_INDEX specifies: ", in_path,
         "\ncurrent column count=", ncol(df_raw), ", H_COL_INDEX=", h_col_index)
  }

  meta_cols_idx    <- 1:(h_col_index - 1)      # A..(H-1)
  feature_cols_idx <- h_col_index:ncol(df_raw) # H..end
  meta_cols        <- names(df_raw)[meta_cols_idx]
  feature_cols     <- names(df_raw)[feature_cols_idx]

  # Processing step.
  df_num <- df_raw
  for (nm in feature_cols) {
    df_num[[nm]] <- to_numeric_soft(df_num[[nm]])
  }

  # Processing step.
  df_unique <- df_num %>%
    distinct(.data[[col_head]], .keep_all = TRUE)

  # Z-score normalization.
  df_unique_norm <- df_unique
  df_unique_norm[feature_cols] <- lapply(df_unique[feature_cols], zscore_vector)

  # Processing step.
  key_norm <- df_unique_norm[, c(col_head, feature_cols), drop = FALSE]

  idx <- match(df_raw[[col_head]], key_norm[[col_head]])
  df_joined <- df_raw
  for (nm in feature_cols) {
    df_joined[[nm]] <- key_norm[[nm]][idx]
  }

  # Processing step.
  df_joined <- df_joined[, original_cols, drop = FALSE]

  # Processing step.
  df_joined_rounded     <- round_numeric_cols(df_joined,     digits = 5)
  df_unique_norm_rounded<- round_numeric_cols(df_unique_norm, digits = 5)

  # Processing step.
  out_name <- sub("\\.(xlsx|csv)$", "", basename(in_path), ignore.case = TRUE)
  out_path <- file.path(out_dir, paste0(out_name, "_normalized_z.xlsx"))

  wb <- createWorkbook()
  addWorksheet(wb, "normalized_z_restored_shape")
  addWorksheet(wb, "deduplicated_used_for_scaling")
  addWorksheet(wb, "info")

  # Z-score normalization.
  writeData(wb, "normalized_z_restored_shape", df_joined_rounded)

  # Z-score normalization.
  writeData(wb, "deduplicated_used_for_scaling", df_unique_norm_rounded)

  # Processing step.
  writeData(
    wb,
    "info",
    data.frame(
      file = in_path,
      headword_col = col_head,
      meta_cols_unchanged_start = 1,
      meta_cols_unchanged_end   = h_col_index - 1,
      normalized_cols_start_idx = h_col_index,
      normalized_cols_end_idx   = ncol(df_raw),
      normalized_cols_start_name= names(df_raw)[h_col_index],
      normalized_cols_end_name  = names(df_raw)[ncol(df_raw)],
      n_rows_original           = nrow(df_raw),
      n_rows_deduplicated       = nrow(df_unique),
      n_features_normalized     = length(feature_cols)
    )
  )

  saveWorkbook(wb, out_path, overwrite = TRUE)
  message("Output written: ", out_path)

  invisible(out_path)
}

# Processing step.
process_one_file(FILE_HOM,  OUT_DIR, H_COL_INDEX)
process_one_file(FILE_MONO, OUT_DIR, H_COL_INDEX)
process_one_file(FILE_POLY, OUT_DIR, H_COL_INDEX)

message("All files completed.")
```

Expected output:
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/03_normaliation_91/*_normalized_z.xlsx`

## Step 04: Select Top-k Cosine-Similarity Candidates

Use normalized 91-feature vectors to retrieve top-5 polyseme and monoseme candidates for each homonym, then enforce unique assignments using gap-based conflict resolution.

```{r step_04}
NORM_HOM  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/03_normaliation_91/homonym_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered_normalized_z.xlsx"
NORM_POLY <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/03_normaliation_91/polyseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered_normalized_z.xlsx"
NORM_MONO <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/03_normaliation_91/monoseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered_normalized_z.xlsx"

OUT_DIR  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/04_cosine_similarity"
OUT_XLSX <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "similarity_topk_cosine_unique_assign_latestNorm.xlsx")

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

##############################################
# Processing step.
##############################################
# install.packages(c("readxl","openxlsx","dplyr","tibble","purrr"))
library(readxl)
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)

##############################################
# Processing step.
##############################################
TOPK <- 5
COL_HEADWORD <- "headword"
H_COL_INDEX <- 8  # Processing step.

##############################################
# Processing step.
##############################################

read_norm_sheet <- function(path, sheet_name = "normalized_z_restored_shape") {
  read_xlsx(path, sheet = sheet_name)
}

extract_feature_matrix <- function(df, headword_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX) {

  # Processing step.
  if (!(headword_col %in% names(df))) {
    nm <- names(df)
    hit <- nm[ trimws(tolower(nm)) == "headword" ]
    if (length(hit) == 0) stop("Could not find the headword column in file: ", headword_col)
    headword_col <- hit[1]
  }

  if (h_col_index > ncol(df)) {
    stop("The file has fewer columns than H_COL_INDEX; check whether H_COL_INDEX is correct.")
  }

  feature_cols  <- names(df)[h_col_index:ncol(df)]

  # Processing step.
  df_head_feats <- df[, c(headword_col, feature_cols), drop = FALSE] %>%
    distinct(.data[[headword_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

  # Processing step.
  names(df_head_feats)[1] <- "headword"

  # Processing step.
  for (j in 2:ncol(df_head_feats)) {
    if (!is.numeric(df_head_feats[[j]])) {
      suppressWarnings({
        df_head_feats[[j]] <- as.numeric(df_head_feats[[j]])
      })
    }
  }

  # Processing step.
  impute_col_median <- function(M){
    for (j in seq_len(ncol(M))) {
      x <- M[, j]
      if (anyNA(x)) {
        med <- suppressWarnings(stats::median(x, na.rm = TRUE))
        if (is.na(med) || is.infinite(med)) med <- 0
        x[is.na(x)] <- med
        M[, j] <- x
      }
    }
    M
  }

  feat_only_mat <- as.matrix(df_head_feats[, -1, drop = FALSE])
  feat_only_mat <- impute_col_median(feat_only_mat)

  list(
    ids = df_head_feats$headword,
    mat = feat_only_mat
  )
}

cosine_sim <- function(A, B){
  A <- as.matrix(A); B <- as.matrix(B)
  An <- sqrt(rowSums(A*A)); Bn <- sqrt(rowSums(B*B))
  An[An==0] <- 1e-12; Bn[Bn==0] <- 1e-12
  (A %*% t(B)) / (An %o% Bn)
}

topk_from_sim <- function(sim_mat, q_ids, c_ids, k = TOPK){
  res <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(sim_mat)), function(i){
    s <- sim_mat[i, ]
    ord <- order(s, decreasing = TRUE)
    kk <- min(k, length(ord))
    tibble(
      query      = q_ids[i],
      rank       = seq_len(kk),
      candidate  = c_ids[ord[seq_len(kk)]],
      similarity = s[ord[seq_len(kk)]]
    )
  })
  bind_rows(res)
}

##########################################################
# Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
##########################################################

calc_gap_for_choice <- function(tk_rows, r){
  cur_sim  <- tk_rows$similarity[ tk_rows$rank == r   ]
  next_sim <- tk_rows$similarity[ tk_rows$rank == r+1 ]
  if (length(cur_sim) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  if (length(next_sim) == 0) return(Inf)
  cur_sim - next_sim
}

##########################################################
# Processing step.
# Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
# Processing step.
##########################################################

resolve_conflicts_greedy_gap <- function(topk_tbl, max_rank = TOPK){

  queries <- unique(topk_tbl$query)

  # Processing step.
  state <- tibble(
    query = queries,
    current_rank_attempt = 1L,  # Processing step.
    assigned_candidate = NA_character_,  # Processing step.
    assigned_similarity = NA_real_,
    assigned_rank       = NA_integer_,
    status              = NA_character_   # primary / fallback / special/no
  )

  # Processing step.
  used_candidates <- character(0)

  still_need_assignment <- function(st){
    st %>%
      filter(is.na(assigned_candidate),
             current_rank_attempt <= max_rank)
  }

  repeat {
    seekers <- still_need_assignment(state)
    if (nrow(seekers) == 0) break

    # Processing step.
    wish_list_rows <- vector("list", nrow(seekers))
    idx <- 0L

    for (i in seq_len(nrow(seekers))) {
      q <- seekers$query[i]
      r <- seekers$current_rank_attempt[i]

      tk_rows <- topk_tbl %>% filter(query == q)
      cand_row <- tk_rows %>% filter(rank == r)

      # Processing step.
      if (nrow(cand_row) == 0) {
        state$current_rank_attempt[state$query == q] <- r + 1L
        next
      }

      cand <- cand_row$candidate[1]
      simv <- cand_row$similarity[1]

      # Processing step.
      if (cand %in% used_candidates) {
        state$current_rank_attempt[state$query == q] <- r + 1L
        next
      }

      g <- calc_gap_for_choice(tk_rows, r)

      idx <- idx + 1L
      wish_list_rows[[idx]] <- tibble(
        query           = q,
        want_rank       = r,
        want_candidate  = cand,
        want_similarity = simv,
        gap             = g
      )
    }

    # Processing step.
    if (idx == 0L) next

    wish_list <- bind_rows(wish_list_rows[seq_len(idx)])

    # Processing step.
    # Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
    resolutions <- wish_list %>%
      group_by(want_candidate) %>%
      arrange(desc(gap), desc(want_similarity), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
      slice(1) %>%
      ungroup() %>%
      mutate(winner_query = query)

    # Processing step.
    wish_list_with_winner <- wish_list %>%
      left_join(resolutions %>% select(want_candidate, winner_query),
                by = "want_candidate") %>%
      mutate(win_flag = (query == winner_query))

    winners_tbl <- wish_list_with_winner %>%
      filter(win_flag) %>%
      select(query,
             assigned_candidate  = want_candidate,
             assigned_similarity = want_similarity,
             assigned_rank       = want_rank)

    losers_tbl <- wish_list_with_winner %>%
      filter(!win_flag) %>%
      select(query, want_rank)

    # Processing step.
    if (nrow(losers_tbl) > 0) {
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(losers_tbl))) {
        q_i <- losers_tbl$query[i]
        r_i <- losers_tbl$want_rank[i]
        state$current_rank_attempt[state$query == q_i] <-
          pmin(max_rank + 1L, r_i + 1L)
      }
    }

    # Processing step.
    if (nrow(winners_tbl) > 0) {
      for (i in seq_len(nrow(winners_tbl))) {
        q_i  <- winners_tbl$query[i]
        cand <- winners_tbl$assigned_candidate[i]
        simv <- winners_tbl$assigned_similarity[i]
        rk   <- winners_tbl$assigned_rank[i]

        state$assigned_candidate[state$query == q_i]  <- cand
        state$assigned_similarity[state$query == q_i] <- simv
        state$assigned_rank[state$query == q_i]       <- rk

        used_candidates <- c(used_candidates, cand)
      }
    }
  }

  # Processing step.
  state <- state %>%
    mutate(
      status = dplyr::case_when(
        !is.na(assigned_candidate) & assigned_rank == 1L ~ "primary",
        !is.na(assigned_candidate) & assigned_rank > 1L  ~ "fallback",
        TRUE                                             ~ "special/no"
      )
    )

  state
}

##############################################
# Processing step.
##############################################

# Processing step.
hom_df  <- read_norm_sheet(NORM_HOM,  sheet_name = "normalized_z_restored_shape")
poly_df <- read_norm_sheet(NORM_POLY, sheet_name = "normalized_z_restored_shape")
mono_df <- read_norm_sheet(NORM_MONO, sheet_name = "normalized_z_restored_shape")

# Processing step.
hom_info  <- extract_feature_matrix(hom_df,  headword_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)
poly_info <- extract_feature_matrix(poly_df, headword_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)
mono_info <- extract_feature_matrix(mono_df, headword_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)

# Processing step.
common_feats <- Reduce(intersect, list(
  colnames(hom_info$mat),
  colnames(poly_info$mat),
  colnames(mono_info$mat)
))
if (length(common_feats) == 0) {
  stop("The three normalized files have no shared feature columns after the H-column boundary.")
}

hom_mat  <- hom_info$mat[,  common_feats, drop = FALSE]
poly_mat <- poly_info$mat[, common_feats, drop = FALSE]
mono_mat <- mono_info$mat[, common_feats, drop = FALSE]

idH <- hom_info$ids
idP <- poly_info$ids
idM <- mono_info$ids

# Cosine similarity and candidate ranking.
cos_hp <- cosine_sim(hom_mat, poly_mat)  # homonym vs polyseme
cos_hm <- cosine_sim(hom_mat, mono_mat)  # homonym vs monoseme

# Candidate-set preparation.
cos_topk_poly <- topk_from_sim(cos_hp, idH, idP, k = TOPK)
cos_topk_mono <- topk_from_sim(cos_hm, idH, idM, k = TOPK)

# Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
poly_assignment_unique <- resolve_conflicts_greedy_gap(cos_topk_poly, max_rank = TOPK)
mono_assignment_unique <- resolve_conflicts_greedy_gap(cos_topk_mono, max_rank = TOPK)

##############################################
# Processing step.
##############################################

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "settings")
addWorksheet(wb, "cos_topk_poly")
addWorksheet(wb, "cos_topk_mono")
addWorksheet(wb, "poly_assignment_unique")
addWorksheet(wb, "mono_assignment_unique")

writeData(
  wb, "settings",
  tibble(
    note = "Cosine-only, TOPK=5. Assignment uses global conflict resolution with gap-based priority (larger gap preferred) and globally unique candidates.",
    topk = TOPK,
    n_homonym = length(idH),
    n_polyseme = length(idP),
    n_monoseme = length(idM),
    n_common_features_used = length(common_feats),
    example_features_head = paste(head(common_feats,10), collapse = ", ")
  )
)

writeData(wb, "cos_topk_poly", cos_topk_poly)
writeData(wb, "cos_topk_mono", cos_topk_mono)
writeData(wb, "poly_assignment_unique", poly_assignment_unique)
writeData(wb, "mono_assignment_unique", mono_assignment_unique)

saveWorkbook(wb, OUT_XLSX, overwrite = TRUE)
message("Completed: ", OUT_XLSX)

```

Expected output:
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/04_cosine_similarity/similarity_topk_cosine_unique_assign_latestNorm.xlsx`

## Step 05: Validate Feature-Level Matching with Raw Welch t-tests

Use the unique cosine assignments and raw 91-feature tables to run feature-wise Welch tests for homonym vs polyseme, homonym vs monoseme, and polyseme vs monoseme.

```{r step_05}
ASSIGN_XLSX <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/04_cosine_similarity/similarity_topk_cosine_unique_assign_latestNorm.xlsx"

# Processing step.
RAW_HOM  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/homonym_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"
RAW_POLY <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/polyseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"
RAW_MONO <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/monoseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"

OUT_DIR  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/05_raw_ttest"
OUT_XLSX <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "feature_level_ttests_hom_vs_poly_mono.xlsx")


dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)


# install.packages(c("readxl","openxlsx","dplyr","tibble","purrr"))
library(readxl)
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(purrr)


COL_HEADWORD <- "headword"
H_COL_INDEX  <- 8  # Processing step.
FDR_METHOD   <- "BH"   # Benjaminiâ€“Hochberg FDR
# Statistical testing step.
# Processing step.

###########################
# Processing step.
###########################

# Processing step.
# Processing step.
# Processing step.
# Processing step.
read_raw_dedup <- function(path, head_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX) {
  df <- read_xlsx(path)

  # Processing step.
  if (!(head_col %in% names(df))) {
    nm  <- names(df)
    hit <- nm[ trimws(tolower(nm)) == "headword" ]
    if (length(hit) == 0) stop("Could not find the headword column in ", path)
    head_col <- hit[1]
  }

  if (h_col_index > ncol(df)) {
    stop("The file has fewer columns than H_COL_INDEX; please check H_COL_INDEX: ", path)
  }

  feature_cols <- names(df)[h_col_index:ncol(df)]

  # Processing step.
  df_dedup <- df %>%
    distinct(.data[[head_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

  # Processing step.
  names(df_dedup)[names(df_dedup) == head_col] <- "headword"

  # Processing step.
  for (fn in feature_cols) {
    if (!is.numeric(df_dedup[[fn]])) {
      suppressWarnings({
        df_dedup[[fn]] <- as.numeric(df_dedup[[fn]])
      })
    }
  }

  list(
    df = df_dedup,
    feature_cols = feature_cols
  )
}

# Processing step.
#    poly_assignment_unique: homonym -> poly
#    mono_assignment_unique: homonym -> mono
read_assignments <- function(path_assign) {

  poly_assign <- read_xlsx(path_assign, sheet = "poly_assignment_unique")
  mono_assign <- read_xlsx(path_assign, sheet = "mono_assignment_unique")

  poly_assign_clean <- poly_assign %>%
    select(query, assigned_candidate, assigned_similarity,
           assigned_rank, status)

  mono_assign_clean <- mono_assign %>%
    select(query, assigned_candidate, assigned_similarity,
           assigned_rank, status)

  list(
    poly = poly_assign_clean,
    mono = mono_assign_clean
  )
}

# Processing step.
# Processing step.
# Processing step.
# Processing step.
# Processing step.
# Processing step.
# Processing step.
# Processing step.
prepare_pairing_data <- function(A_df, B_df, assignment_tbl, feature_cols_A, feature_cols_B) {

  assign_ok <- assignment_tbl %>%
    filter(!is.na(assigned_candidate),
           status != "special/no") %>%
    distinct(query, assigned_candidate, .keep_all = TRUE)

  # Processing step.
  assign_ok <- assign_ok %>%
    semi_join(A_df %>% select(headword), by = c("query" = "headword")) %>%
    semi_join(B_df %>% select(headword), by = c("assigned_candidate" = "headword"))

  feats_common <- intersect(feature_cols_A, feature_cols_B)

  list(
    pair_table = assign_ok,
    feature_cols_common = feats_common
  )
}

# Statistical testing step.
# Candidate-set preparation.
# Candidate-set preparation.
# Processing step.
ttest_one_feature <- function(feature_name, pairing_info, A_df, B_df) {

  pairs_tbl <- pairing_info$pair_table

  # Processing step.
  A_vals <- A_df %>%
    select(headword, !!feature_name) %>%
    rename(Aval = !!feature_name)

  # Processing step.
  B_vals <- B_df %>%
    select(headword, !!feature_name) %>%
    rename(Bval = !!feature_name)

  merged <- pairs_tbl %>%
    left_join(A_vals, by = c("query" = "headword")) %>%
    left_join(B_vals, by = c("assigned_candidate" = "headword"))

  merged2 <- merged %>%
    filter(is.finite(Aval), is.finite(Bval))

  x <- merged2$Aval
  y <- merged2$Bval

  # Processing step.
  if (length(x) < 2 || length(y) < 2) {
    return(tibble(
      feature    = feature_name,
      n_A        = length(x),
      n_B        = length(y),
      mean_A     = ifelse(length(x)>0, mean(x), NA_real_),
      mean_B     = ifelse(length(y)>0, mean(y), NA_real_),
      diff_mean  = ifelse(length(x)>0 && length(y)>0, mean(x)-mean(y), NA_real_),
      t_stat     = NA_real_,
      df         = NA_real_,
      p_val      = NA_real_
    ))
  }

  tt <- tryCatch(
    t.test(x, y, var.equal = FALSE),  # Processing step.
    error = function(e) NULL
  )

  if (is.null(tt)) {
    tibble(
      feature    = feature_name,
      n_A        = length(x),
      n_B        = length(y),
      mean_A     = mean(x),
      mean_B     = mean(y),
      diff_mean  = mean(x) - mean(y),
      t_stat     = NA_real_,
      df         = NA_real_,
      p_val      = NA_real_
    )
  } else {
    tibble(
      feature    = feature_name,
      n_A        = length(x),
      n_B        = length(y),
      mean_A     = mean(x),
      mean_B     = mean(y),
      diff_mean  = mean(x) - mean(y),
      t_stat     = unname(tt$statistic),
      df         = unname(tt$parameter),
      p_val      = unname(tt$p.value)
    )
  }
}

# Statistical testing step.
run_all_ttests <- function(pairing_info, A_df, B_df, fdr_method = "BH",
                           label_A = "group_A", label_B = "group_B") {

  feats <- pairing_info$feature_cols_common

  rows <- lapply(feats, function(fnm) {
    ttest_one_feature(
      feature_name  = fnm,
      pairing_info  = pairing_info,
      A_df          = A_df,
      B_df          = B_df
    )
  })

  out_tbl <- bind_rows(rows) %>%
    mutate(
      p_adj_BH = p.adjust(p_val, method = fdr_method),
      sig_FDR_0_05 = p_adj_BH < 0.05,
      group_A = label_A,
      group_B = label_B
    ) %>%
    select(feature,
           group_A, group_B,
           n_A, n_B,
           mean_A, mean_B, diff_mean,
           t_stat, df, p_val,
           p_adj_BH, sig_FDR_0_05)

  out_tbl
}

###########################
# Processing step.
###########################

# Candidate-set preparation.
assign_list <- read_assignments(ASSIGN_XLSX)
poly_assign_tbl <- assign_list$poly  # columns: query, assigned_candidate, status, ...
mono_assign_tbl <- assign_list$mono  # columns: query, assigned_candidate, status, ...

# Processing step.
hom_raw_info  <- read_raw_dedup(RAW_HOM,  head_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)
poly_raw_info <- read_raw_dedup(RAW_POLY, head_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)
mono_raw_info <- read_raw_dedup(RAW_MONO, head_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)

hom_df_dedup  <- hom_raw_info$df
poly_df_dedup <- poly_raw_info$df
mono_df_dedup <- mono_raw_info$df

hom_feats  <- hom_raw_info$feature_cols
poly_feats <- poly_raw_info$feature_cols
mono_feats <- mono_raw_info$feature_cols

# 3. homonym vs polyseme
pair_hom_poly <- prepare_pairing_data(
  A_df = hom_df_dedup,
  B_df = poly_df_dedup,
  assignment_tbl = poly_assign_tbl,
  feature_cols_A = hom_feats,
  feature_cols_B = poly_feats
)
ttest_hom_vs_poly <- run_all_ttests(
  pairing_info = pair_hom_poly,
  A_df = hom_df_dedup,
  B_df = poly_df_dedup,
  fdr_method = FDR_METHOD,
  label_A = "homonym",
  label_B = "polyseme"
)

# 4. homonym vs monoseme
pair_hom_mono <- prepare_pairing_data(
  A_df = hom_df_dedup,
  B_df = mono_df_dedup,
  assignment_tbl = mono_assign_tbl,
  feature_cols_A = hom_feats,
  feature_cols_B = mono_feats
)
ttest_hom_vs_mono <- run_all_ttests(
  pairing_info = pair_hom_mono,
  A_df = hom_df_dedup,
  B_df = mono_df_dedup,
  fdr_method = FDR_METHOD,
  label_A = "homonym",
  label_B = "monoseme"
)

# Candidate-set preparation.
# Candidate-set preparation.
# Candidate-set preparation.
# Candidate-set preparation.
# Candidate-set preparation.
#
# Processing step.
# Candidate-set preparation.
# Candidate-set preparation.
#
poly_mono_assign_tbl <- poly_assign_tbl %>%
  filter(!is.na(assigned_candidate),
         status != "special/no") %>%
  select(query, poly_candidate = assigned_candidate)

mono_mono_assign_tbl <- mono_assign_tbl %>%
  filter(!is.na(assigned_candidate),
         status != "special/no") %>%
  select(query, mono_candidate = assigned_candidate)

# Candidate-set preparation.
poly_vs_mono_pairs <- poly_mono_assign_tbl %>%
  inner_join(mono_mono_assign_tbl, by = "query") %>%
  distinct(query, poly_candidate, mono_candidate)

# Processing step.
# query -> assigned_candidate
poly_vs_mono_assignment_like <- poly_vs_mono_pairs %>%
  transmute(query = poly_candidate,
            assigned_candidate = mono_candidate,
            status = "OK")  # dummy status so it passes filter

pair_poly_mono <- prepare_pairing_data(
  A_df = poly_df_dedup,
  B_df = mono_df_dedup,
  assignment_tbl = poly_vs_mono_assignment_like,
  feature_cols_A = poly_feats,
  feature_cols_B = mono_feats
)

ttest_poly_vs_mono <- run_all_ttests(
  pairing_info = pair_poly_mono,
  A_df = poly_df_dedup,
  B_df = mono_df_dedup,
  fdr_method = FDR_METHOD,
  label_A = "polyseme",
  label_B = "monoseme"
)

ttest_hom_vs_poly <- ttest_hom_vs_poly %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 5)))
ttest_hom_vs_mono <- ttest_hom_vs_mono %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 5)))
ttest_poly_vs_mono <- ttest_poly_vs_mono %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 5)))


###########################
# Processing step.
###########################

wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "settings")
addWorksheet(wb, "ttest_hom_vs_poly")
addWorksheet(wb, "ttest_hom_vs_mono")
addWorksheet(wb, "ttest_poly_vs_mono")
addWorksheet(wb, "pair_used_hom_poly")
addWorksheet(wb, "pair_used_hom_mono")
addWorksheet(wb, "pair_used_poly_mono")


writeData(
  wb, "settings",
  tibble(
    note = "Welch t-tests per feature comparing homonym vs polyseme, homonym vs monoseme, and polyseme vs monoseme. Data = RAW (features_saved_filtered). Pairs defined by unique cosine assignment; FDR = BH.",
    H_COL_INDEX = H_COL_INDEX,
    n_pair_hom_poly  = nrow(pair_hom_poly$pair_table),
    n_pair_hom_mono  = nrow(pair_hom_mono$pair_table),
    n_pair_poly_mono = nrow(pair_poly_mono$pair_table),
    n_feat_hom_poly  = length(pair_hom_poly$feature_cols_common),
    n_feat_hom_mono  = length(pair_hom_mono$feature_cols_common),
    n_feat_poly_mono = length(pair_poly_mono$feature_cols_common)
  )
)



writeData(wb, "ttest_hom_vs_poly", ttest_hom_vs_poly)
writeData(wb, "ttest_hom_vs_mono", ttest_hom_vs_mono)
writeData(wb, "ttest_poly_vs_mono", ttest_poly_vs_mono)


writeData(wb, "pair_used_hom_poly",  pair_hom_poly$pair_table)
writeData(wb, "pair_used_hom_mono",  pair_hom_mono$pair_table)
writeData(wb, "pair_used_poly_mono", pair_poly_mono$pair_table)

saveWorkbook(wb, OUT_XLSX, overwrite = TRUE)
message("Output written: ", OUT_XLSX)

```

Expected output:
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/05_raw_ttest/feature_level_ttests_hom_vs_poly_mono.xlsx`

## Step 06: Build Sense-Count Summary with Polyseme and Monoseme Backups

First compute baseline sense counts for cosine assignments, then refine the candidate set by adding unique 3-sense polyseme replacements and monoseme backup candidates.

```{r step_06}
ASSIGN_XLSX <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/04_cosine_similarity/similarity_topk_cosine_unique_assign_latestNorm.xlsx"

FILE_HOM_SENSE  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/homonym_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"
FILE_POLY_SENSE <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/polyseme_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"
FILE_MONO_SENSE <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/monoseme_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"

OUT_DIR  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/06_sense_counts"
OUT_XLSX <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "sense_counts_summary.xlsx")

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Processing step.
# install.packages(c("readxl","openxlsx","dplyr","tibble"))
library(readxl)
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
get_colname_loose <- function(df, target_name){
    nm  <- names(df)
    m   <- trimws(tolower(nm))
    hit <- nm[m == tolower(target_name)]
    if (length(hit) == 0) {
        stop(
            "Could not find required column: ", target_name,
            " current columns: ", paste(names(df), collapse=", ")
        )
    }
    hit[1]
}

# Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
sense_count_per_word <- function(path_excel){
    df <- read_xlsx(path_excel)

    # Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
    col_hw    <- get_colname_loose(df, "headword")
    col_sense <- get_colname_loose(df, "sense1")

    # Processing step.
    tmp <- df %>%
        mutate(
            .hw    = .data[[col_hw]],
            .sense = .data[[col_sense]]
        ) %>%
        # Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
        filter(
            !is.na(.hw),
            !is.na(.sense),
            trimws(.sense) != ""
        ) %>%
        distinct(.hw, .sense)

    # Processing step.
    out <- tmp %>%
        group_by(.hw) %>%
        summarise(sense_count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
        rename(headword = .hw)

    out
}

# Processing step.
assign_poly <- read_xlsx(ASSIGN_XLSX, sheet = "poly_assignment_unique")
assign_mono <- read_xlsx(ASSIGN_XLSX, sheet = "mono_assignment_unique")

assign_poly_clean <- assign_poly %>%
    select(query,
           assigned_candidate,
           assigned_similarity,
           assigned_rank,
           status)

assign_mono_clean <- assign_mono %>%
    select(query,
           assigned_candidate,
           assigned_similarity,
           assigned_rank,
           status)

# Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
hom_sense_tbl  <- sense_count_per_word(FILE_HOM_SENSE)  # Candidate-set preparation.
poly_sense_tbl <- sense_count_per_word(FILE_POLY_SENSE)  # Candidate-set preparation.
mono_sense_tbl <- sense_count_per_word(FILE_MONO_SENSE)  # Candidate-set preparation.

# Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.

## 3A. homonym vs polyseme
poly_pairs_with_sense <- assign_poly_clean %>%
    # Candidate-set preparation.
    left_join(
        hom_sense_tbl %>%
            rename(homonym = headword,
                   homonym_sense_number = sense_count),
        by = c("query" = "homonym")
    ) %>%
    # Candidate-set preparation.
    left_join(
        poly_sense_tbl %>%
            rename(polyseme_candidate = headword,
                   polyseme_candidate_sense_number = sense_count),
        by = c("assigned_candidate" = "polyseme_candidate")
    ) %>%
    transmute(
        homonym = query,
        homonym_sense_number,
        polyseme_candidate = assigned_candidate,
        polyseme_candidate_sense_number,
        assigned_similarity,
        assigned_rank,
        status
    )

## 3B. homonym vs monoseme
mono_pairs_with_sense <- assign_mono_clean %>%
    # Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
    left_join(
        hom_sense_tbl %>%
            rename(homonym = headword,
                   homonym_sense_number = sense_count),
        by = c("query" = "homonym")
    ) %>%
    # Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
    left_join(
        mono_sense_tbl %>%
            rename(monoseme_candidate = headword,
                   monoseme_candidate_sense_number = sense_count),
        by = c("assigned_candidate" = "monoseme_candidate")
    ) %>%
    transmute(
        homonym = query,
        homonym_sense_number,
        monoseme_candidate = assigned_candidate,
        monoseme_candidate_sense_number,
        assigned_similarity,
        assigned_rank,
        status
    )

# Processing step.
poly_pairs_with_sense <- poly_pairs_with_sense %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 5)))
mono_pairs_with_sense <- mono_pairs_with_sense %>%
    mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 5)))

# Processing step.
wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "settings")
addWorksheet(wb, "homonym_vs_polyseme")
addWorksheet(wb, "homonym_vs_monoseme")

writeData(
    wb, "settings",
    tibble(
        note = "For each homonym and its assigned polyseme/monoseme (from cosine gap-based assignment), we count unique sense1 per word from *_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx.",
        n_pairs_poly = nrow(poly_pairs_with_sense),
        n_pairs_mono = nrow(mono_pairs_with_sense)
    )
)

writeData(wb, "homonym_vs_polyseme", poly_pairs_with_sense)
writeData(wb, "homonym_vs_monoseme", mono_pairs_with_sense)

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
saveWorkbook(wb, OUT_XLSX, overwrite = TRUE)
message("Output written: ", OUT_XLSX)


# ---- Final sense-balanced candidate refinement ----
ASSIGN_XLSX <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/04_cosine_similarity/similarity_topk_cosine_unique_assign_latestNorm.xlsx"
SENSE_XLSX  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/06_sense_counts/sense_counts_summary.xlsx"

# Cosine similarity and candidate ranking.
NORM_HOM  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/03_normaliation_91/homonym_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered_normalized_z.xlsx"
NORM_POLY <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/03_normaliation_91/polyseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered_normalized_z.xlsx"

# Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
POLY_PREPROC <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/polyseme_data_preprocess.xlsx"  # sheet: sense_example_grid

OUT_DIR  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/06_sense_counts"
OUT_XLSX <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "sense_counts_summary_WITH_poly3_mono_backups.xlsx")

# Processing step.
COL_HEADWORD <- "headword"
H_COL_INDEX  <- 8
digits_keep  <- 5

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Processing step.
# install.packages(c("readxl","openxlsx","dplyr","tibble","stringr","purrr"))
library(readxl); library(openxlsx); library(dplyr); library(tibble); library(stringr); library(purrr)

# Processing step.
round5 <- function(x) round(x, digits_keep)

read_norm_sheet <- function(path, sheet = "normalized_z_restored_shape") {
  read_xlsx(path, sheet = sheet)
}

# Processing step.
extract_feature_matrix <- function(df, headword_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX){
  if (!(headword_col %in% names(df))) {
    nm <- names(df)
    hit <- nm[ trimws(tolower(nm)) == "headword" ]
    if (length(hit) == 0) stop("Could not find the headword column.")
    headword_col <- hit[1]
  }
  if (h_col_index > ncol(df)) stop("H_COL_INDEX exceeds the number of columns.")

  feature_cols <- names(df)[h_col_index:ncol(df)]
  df_hf <- df[, c(headword_col, feature_cols), drop = FALSE] %>%
    distinct(.data[[headword_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)
  names(df_hf)[1] <- "headword"
  for (j in 2:ncol(df_hf)) {
    if (!is.numeric(df_hf[[j]])) suppressWarnings(df_hf[[j]] <- as.numeric(df_hf[[j]]))
  }
  # Processing step.
  for (j in 2:ncol(df_hf)) {
    x <- df_hf[[j]]
    if (anyNA(x)) {
      med <- suppressWarnings(stats::median(x, na.rm = TRUE))
      if (is.na(med) || is.infinite(med)) med <- 0
      x[is.na(x)] <- med
      df_hf[[j]] <- x
    }
  }
  list(
    ids = df_hf$headword,
    mat = as.matrix(df_hf[, -1, drop = FALSE]),
    feat_names = colnames(df_hf)[-1]
  )
}

cosine_sim <- function(A, B){
  A <- as.matrix(A); B <- as.matrix(B)
  An <- sqrt(rowSums(A*A)); Bn <- sqrt(rowSums(B*B))
  An[An==0] <- 1e-12; Bn[Bn==0] <- 1e-12
  (A %*% t(B)) / (An %o% Bn)
}

# Candidate-set preparation.
read_cos_topk_mono <- function(assign_xlsx){
  read_xlsx(assign_xlsx, sheet = "cos_topk_mono") %>%
    select(query, rank, candidate, similarity)
}

# Sense-count matching and candidate refinement.
poly3_from_preproc <- function(path_preproc){
  df <- read_xlsx(path_preproc, sheet = "sense_example_grid")
  if (!all(c("sense_category_number","all_words") %in% names(df))) {
    stop("polyseme_data_preprocess.xlsx is missing required columns: sense_category_number or all_words")
  }
  w <- df %>% filter(as.integer(sense_category_number) == 3) %>%
    pull(all_words) %>% as.character()
  if (length(w) == 0) return(character(0))
  words <- unlist(str_split(paste(w, collapse = " "), pattern = "[,ï¼Œã€\\s]+"))
  words <- unique(words[nzchar(words)])
  words
}

# Candidate-set preparation.
reorder_by_vector <- function(tbl, col_name, order_vec){
  tbl %>%
    mutate(.ord_factor = factor(.data[[col_name]], levels = order_vec)) %>%
    arrange(.ord_factor) %>%
    select(-.ord_factor)
}

# Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
rank_3sense_for_one <- function(h_vec, P3mat, poly3_words){
  hn <- sqrt(sum(h_vec*h_vec)); if (hn == 0) hn <- 1e-12
  pn <- sqrt(rowSums(P3mat*P3mat)); pn[pn==0] <- 1e-12
  simv <- as.vector(h_vec %*% t(P3mat)) / (hn * pn)
  ord  <- order(simv, decreasing = TRUE)
  tibble(
    candidate = poly3_words[ord],
    sim       = simv[ord],
    rank      = seq_along(ord)
  )
}

# Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
assign_unique_poly3 <- function(target_rows, Hmat, Hids, P3mat, poly3_words, banned_words){
  if (nrow(target_rows) == 0) {
    return(tibble(
      homonym = character(0),
      polyseme_3sense_top1 = character(0),
      polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity = numeric(0),
      polyseme_3sense_top1_sense_number = integer(0)
    ))
  }
  homs <- target_rows$homonym

  ranked_list <- list()
  ptr_idx     <- integer(length(homs))
  names(ptr_idx) <- homs

  for (k in seq_along(homs)) {
    hh <- homs[k]
    i  <- match(hh, Hids)
    if (is.na(i)) {
      ranked_list[[hh]] <- tibble(candidate = character(0), sim = numeric(0), rank = integer(0))
      ptr_idx[hh] <- NA_integer_
      next
    }
    hrow <- matrix(Hmat[i, , drop = TRUE], nrow = 1)
    rl0 <- {
      hn <- sqrt(sum(hrow*hrow)); if (hn == 0) hn <- 1e-12
      pn <- sqrt(rowSums(P3mat*P3mat)); pn[pn==0] <- 1e-12
      simv <- as.vector(hrow %*% t(P3mat)) / (hn * pn)
      ord  <- order(simv, decreasing = TRUE)
      tibble(candidate = poly3_words[ord], sim = simv[ord], rank = seq_along(ord))
    }
    if (length(banned_words) > 0) {
      rl0 <- rl0 %>% dplyr::filter(!(candidate %in% banned_words))
    }
    ranked_list[[hh]] <- rl0
    ptr_idx[hh] <- if (nrow(rl0) >= 1) 1L else NA_integer_
  }

  current_choice <- function(hh){
    idx <- ptr_idx[hh]
    rl  <- ranked_list[[hh]]
    if (is.na(idx) || idx < 1 || idx > nrow(rl)) {
      return(list(cand = NA_character_, sim = NA_real_, gap = NA_real_))
    }
    sim1 <- rl$sim[idx]
    sim2 <- if (idx + 1 <= nrow(rl)) rl$sim[idx + 1] else -Inf
    gap  <- sim1 - sim2
    list(cand = rl$candidate[idx], sim = sim1, gap = gap)
  }

  repeat {
    assign_tbl <- lapply(homs, function(hh){
      ch <- current_choice(hh)
      tibble(homonym = hh, cand = ch$cand, sim = ch$sim, gap = ch$gap)
    }) %>% dplyr::bind_rows()

    if (is.list(assign_tbl$cand)) {
      assign_tbl <- assign_tbl %>%
        mutate(cand = vapply(cand,
                             function(z) if (length(z)) as.character(z[[1]]) else NA_character_,
                             FUN.VALUE = character(1)))
    } else {
      assign_tbl <- assign_tbl %>% mutate(cand = as.character(cand))
    }

    if (all(is.na(assign_tbl$cand))) break

    conflict_keys <- assign_tbl %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(cand)) %>%
      dplyr::count(cand, name = "n") %>%
      dplyr::filter(n > 1) %>%
      dplyr::pull(cand)

    if (length(conflict_keys) == 0) break

    for (ck in conflict_keys) {
      comp <- assign_tbl %>% dplyr::filter(cand == ck)
      win_row <- comp %>%
        dplyr::arrange(dplyr::desc(gap), dplyr::desc(sim), match(homonym, homs)) %>%
        dplyr::slice(1)
      keep_h <- win_row$homonym[1]

      losers <- setdiff(comp$homonym, keep_h)
      for (hh in losers) {
        idx <- ptr_idx[hh]
        if (!is.na(idx)) {
          ptr_idx[hh] <- idx + 1L
          rl <- ranked_list[[hh]]
          if (is.na(ptr_idx[hh]) || ptr_idx[hh] > nrow(rl)) ptr_idx[hh] <- NA_integer_
        }
      }
    }
  }

  out <- lapply(homs, function(hh){
    ch <- current_choice(hh)
    tibble(
      homonym = hh,
      polyseme_3sense_top1 = ch$cand,
      polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity = ch$sim,
      polyseme_3sense_top1_sense_number = ifelse(is.na(ch$cand), NA_integer_, 3L)
    )
  }) %>% dplyr::bind_rows() %>%
    dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(where(is.numeric), round5))

  out
}

# Processing step.
poly_sheet <- read_xlsx(SENSE_XLSX, sheet = "homonym_vs_polyseme")
mono_sheet <- read_xlsx(SENSE_XLSX, sheet = "homonym_vs_monoseme")

poly_orig_cols <- names(poly_sheet)
mono_orig_cols <- names(mono_sheet)

hom_order_poly <- poly_sheet$homonym
hom_order_mono <- mono_sheet$homonym

# Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
message("A) Polyseme step: select 3-sense polyseme candidates for rows with two senses and compute similarity_gap ...")

hom_df_norm  <- read_norm_sheet(NORM_HOM,  "normalized_z_restored_shape")
poly_df_norm <- read_norm_sheet(NORM_POLY, "normalized_z_restored_shape")
hom_info  <- extract_feature_matrix(hom_df_norm,  COL_HEADWORD, H_COL_INDEX)
poly_info <- extract_feature_matrix(poly_df_norm, COL_HEADWORD, H_COL_INDEX)

common_feats <- intersect(colnames(hom_info$mat), colnames(poly_info$mat))
if (length(common_feats) == 0) stop("The normalized homonym and polyseme tables have no shared feature columns.")
Hmat <- hom_info$mat[,  common_feats, drop = FALSE]
Pmat <- poly_info$mat[, common_feats, drop = FALSE]
Hids <- hom_info$ids
Pids <- poly_info$ids

poly3_words <- poly3_from_preproc(POLY_PREPROC)
poly3_words <- intersect(poly3_words, Pids)
if (length(poly3_words) == 0) {
  warning("No 3-sense polyseme was found in the normalized polyseme table; skipping appended poly3 columns.")
}

banned_words <- unique(na.omit(as.character(poly_sheet$polyseme_candidate)))

if (length(poly3_words) > 0) {
  target_rows <- poly_sheet %>%
    transmute(homonym, polyseme_candidate_sense_number) %>%
    filter(!is.na(polyseme_candidate_sense_number),
           as.integer(polyseme_candidate_sense_number) == 2L)

  idx_poly3 <- match(poly3_words, Pids)
  P3mat <- Pmat[idx_poly3, , drop = FALSE]

  chosen_df <- assign_unique_poly3(target_rows, Hmat, Hids, P3mat, poly3_words, banned_words)

  poly_sheet <- poly_sheet %>%
    left_join(chosen_df, by = "homonym")
  new_cols <- setdiff(names(poly_sheet), poly_orig_cols)
  poly_sheet <- poly_sheet[, c(poly_orig_cols, new_cols), drop = FALSE]
}

poly_sheet <- reorder_by_vector(poly_sheet, "homonym", hom_order_poly)

# Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
if ("assigned_similarity" %in% names(poly_sheet)) {
  poly_sheet$assigned_similarity <- as.numeric(poly_sheet$assigned_similarity)
} else {
  stop("poly_sheet does not contain assigned_similarity.")
}

if ("polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity" %in% names(poly_sheet)) {
  poly_sheet$polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity <- as.numeric(poly_sheet$polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity)
} else {
  stop("poly_sheet does not contain polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity.")
}

poly_sheet <- poly_sheet %>%
  mutate(
    polyseme_candidate_sense_number = as.integer(polyseme_candidate_sense_number),
    polyseme_3sense_top1_sense_number = as.integer(polyseme_3sense_top1_sense_number),
    similarity_gap = if_else(
      !is.na(polyseme_candidate_sense_number) &
        !is.na(polyseme_3sense_top1_sense_number) &
        polyseme_candidate_sense_number == 2L &
        polyseme_3sense_top1_sense_number == 3L &
        !is.na(assigned_similarity) &
        !is.na(polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity),
      round5(assigned_similarity - polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity),
      as.numeric(NA)
    )
  )

poly_with_idx <- poly_sheet %>%
  mutate(.row_id = dplyr::row_number())

gap_candidates_poly <- poly_with_idx %>%
  filter(!is.na(similarity_gap)) %>%
  arrange(similarity_gap)

n_pick_poly <- min(58L, nrow(gap_candidates_poly))

poly_sheet <- poly_sheet %>%
  mutate(
    polyseme_candidate_instead = NA_character_,
    sequence_number = NA_integer_,
    polyseme_candidate_instead_whole = NA_character_
  )

if (n_pick_poly == 0L) {
  warning("No similarity_gap is available for the polyseme step; skipping marking.")
} else {
  pick_rows_poly <- gap_candidates_poly$.row_id[seq_len(n_pick_poly)]
  message("poly: marking ", n_pick_poly, " rows with replacement candidates and sequence numbers.")

  poly_sheet$polyseme_candidate_instead[pick_rows_poly] <- poly_sheet$polyseme_3sense_top1[pick_rows_poly]
  poly_sheet$sequence_number[pick_rows_poly] <- seq_len(n_pick_poly)
}

poly_sheet$polyseme_candidate_instead_whole <- ifelse(
  !is.na(poly_sheet$polyseme_candidate_instead),
  poly_sheet$polyseme_candidate_instead,
  poly_sheet$polyseme_candidate
)

# Candidate-set preparation.
message("B) Monoseme step: add two backup candidates and select alt2_word_other using the smallest alt1-alt2 similarity gap ...")

cos_topk_mono <- read_cos_topk_mono(ASSIGN_XLSX) %>%
  mutate(similarity = round5(similarity))

used_mono <- unique(mono_sheet$monoseme_candidate)
hom_list <- mono_sheet$homonym

pick_two_backups <- function(hh) {
  tk <- cos_topk_mono %>%
    filter(query == hh) %>%
    arrange(rank)
  ranks_pref <- c(2,3,4,5)
  alt <- list()
  for (r in ranks_pref) {
    cand <- tk %>% filter(rank == r)
    if (nrow(cand) == 0) next
    cand_word <- cand$candidate[1]
    if (!(cand_word %in% used_mono)) {
      alt[[length(alt)+1]] <- tibble(
        homonym = hh,
        alt_word = cand_word,
        alt_rank = cand$rank[1],
        alt_similarity = cand$similarity[1]
      )
      used_mono <<- c(used_mono, cand_word)
      if (length(alt) == 2) break
    }
  }
  if (length(alt) == 0) {
    tibble(homonym = hh,
           alt1_word = NA_character_, alt1_rank = NA_integer_, alt1_similarity = NA_real_,
           alt2_word = NA_character_, alt2_rank = NA_integer_, alt2_similarity = NA_real_)
  } else if (length(alt) == 1) {
    a1 <- alt[[1]]
    tibble(homonym = hh,
           alt1_word = a1$alt_word, alt1_rank = a1$alt_rank, alt1_similarity = a1$alt_similarity,
           alt2_word = NA_character_, alt2_rank = NA_integer_, alt2_similarity = NA_real_)
  } else {
    a1 <- alt[[1]]; a2 <- alt[[2]]
    tibble(homonym = hh,
           alt1_word = a1$alt_word, alt1_rank = a1$alt_rank, alt1_similarity = a1$alt_similarity,
           alt2_word = a2$alt_word, alt2_rank = a2$alt_rank, alt2_similarity = a2$alt_similarity)
  }
}

mono_backups <- bind_rows(lapply(hom_list, pick_two_backups)) %>%
  mutate(
    alt1_similarity = round5(alt1_similarity),
    alt2_similarity = round5(alt2_similarity),
    alt1_low_sim_flag = ifelse(!is.na(alt1_similarity) & alt1_similarity < 0.5, TRUE, FALSE),
    alt2_low_sim_flag = ifelse(!is.na(alt2_similarity) & alt2_similarity < 0.5, TRUE, FALSE)
  )

mono_sheet <- mono_sheet %>%
  left_join(mono_backups, by = "homonym") %>%
  reorder_by_vector("homonym", hom_order_mono)
new_cols_mono <- setdiff(names(mono_sheet), mono_orig_cols)
mono_sheet <- mono_sheet[, c(mono_orig_cols, new_cols_mono), drop = FALSE]

mono_two_backups <- mono_backups %>% reorder_by_vector("homonym", hom_order_mono)

# Processing step.
mono_sheet <- mono_sheet %>%
  mutate(` ` = NA_character_)

# Processing step.
mono_sheet <- mono_sheet %>%
  mutate(
    alt1_word_all = alt1_word,
    alt2_word_other = NA_character_,
    similarity_gap_mono = NA_real_,
    alt2_sequence_number = NA_integer_
  )

# Processing step.
mono_with_idx <- mono_sheet %>%
  mutate(.row_id = dplyr::row_number())

gap_candidates_mono <- mono_with_idx %>%
  filter(!is.na(alt1_similarity),
         !is.na(alt2_similarity),
         !is.na(alt2_rank),
         alt2_rank == 3L) %>%
  mutate(
    gap_val = alt1_similarity - alt2_similarity
  ) %>%
  arrange(gap_val)  # Processing step.

n_pick_mono <- min(83L, nrow(gap_candidates_mono))

if (n_pick_mono == 0L) {
  warning("Monoseme step: no rows meet the alt2_rank == 3 and non-missing similarity criteria.")
} else {
  pick_rows_mono <- gap_candidates_mono$.row_id[seq_len(n_pick_mono)]
  message("mono: marking ", n_pick_mono, " rows with alt2 backups, similarity gaps, and sequence numbers.")

  # Processing step.
  mono_sheet$alt2_word_other[pick_rows_mono] <- mono_sheet$alt2_word[pick_rows_mono]
  # Gap-based conflict resolution and uniqueness handling.
  mono_sheet$similarity_gap_mono[pick_rows_mono] <- round5(
    mono_sheet$alt1_similarity[pick_rows_mono] - mono_sheet$alt2_similarity[pick_rows_mono]
  )
  # alt2_sequence_numberï¼š1..n_pick_mono
  mono_sheet$alt2_sequence_number[pick_rows_mono] <- seq_len(n_pick_mono)
}

# Processing step.
wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "settings")
addWorksheet(wb, "homonym_vs_polyseme")
addWorksheet(wb, "homonym_vs_monoseme")
addWorksheet(wb, "mono_two_backups")

writeData(wb, "settings", tibble(
  note = paste0(
    "poly: assign unique 3-sense polyseme candidates to rows whose original polyseme candidate has two senses; ",
    "compute similarity_gap = assigned_similarity - polyseme_3sense_top1_similarity; ",
    "mark the smallest-gap rows with polyseme_candidate_instead, sequence_number, and polyseme_candidate_instead_whole. ",
    "mono: add alt1/alt2 backup candidates, copy alt1_word into alt1_word_all, and mark selected alt2_word_other rows using the smallest alt1-alt2 similarity gaps."
  ),
  norm_hom = NORM_HOM,
  norm_poly = NORM_POLY,
  poly_preproc = POLY_PREPROC,
  assign_xlsx = ASSIGN_XLSX,
  source_sense_xlsx = SENSE_XLSX,
  common_feature_count_used = length(common_feats),
  n_gap_pick_poly = n_pick_poly,
  n_gap_pick_mono = n_pick_mono
))

writeData(wb, "homonym_vs_polyseme", poly_sheet)
writeData(wb, "homonym_vs_monoseme", mono_sheet)
writeData(wb, "mono_two_backups", mono_two_backups)

saveWorkbook(wb, OUT_XLSX, overwrite = TRUE)
message("Output written: ", OUT_XLSX)

```

Expected output:
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/06_sense_counts/sense_counts_summary.xlsx`
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/06_sense_counts/sense_counts_summary_WITH_poly3_mono_backups.xlsx`

## Step 07: Run Final Welch and Wilcoxon Tests on Selected Candidate Sets

Use the final sense-balanced candidate summary and the raw 91-feature tables to run the final Welch and Wilcoxon comparison suite.

```{r step_07}
SUMMARY_XLSX <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/06_sense_counts/sense_counts_summary_WITH_poly3_mono_backups.xlsx"

RAW_HOM  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/homonym_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"
RAW_POLY <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/polyseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"
RAW_MONO <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/02_91_features_saved/monoseme_pos_sense_features_91saved_filtered.xlsx"

OUT_DIR  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/07_final_ttest"
OUT_WELCH  <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "final_Welch_candidate_words_ttest.xlsx")
OUT_WILCOX <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "final_Wilcoxon_candidate_words_ttest.xlsx")

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

#####################
# Processing step.
#####################

COL_HEADWORD <- "headword"
H_COL_INDEX  <- 8
FDR_METHOD   <- "BH"

round5 <- function(x) round(x, 5)

#####################
# Processing step.
#####################

# Processing step.
read_raw_dedup <- function(path, head_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX) {
  df <- read_xlsx(path)

  # Processing step.
  if (!(head_col %in% names(df))) {
    nm  <- names(df)
    hit <- nm[ trimws(tolower(nm)) == "headword" ]
    if (length(hit) == 0) stop("Could not find the headword column in ", path)
    head_col <- hit[1]
  }

  if (h_col_index > ncol(df)) {
    stop("The file has fewer columns than H_COL_INDEX; please check: ", path)
  }

  feature_cols <- names(df)[h_col_index:ncol(df)]

  # Processing step.
  df_dedup <- df %>%
    distinct(.data[[head_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)

  # Processing step.
  names(df_dedup)[names(df_dedup) == head_col] <- "headword"

  # Processing step.
  for (fn in feature_cols) {
    if (!is.numeric(df_dedup[[fn]])) {
      suppressWarnings({
        df_dedup[[fn]] <- as.numeric(df_dedup[[fn]])
      })
    }
  }

  list(
    df = df_dedup,
    feature_cols = feature_cols
  )
}

# Processing step.
get_common_features <- function(hom_feats, poly_feats, mono_feats) {
  Reduce(intersect, list(hom_feats, poly_feats, mono_feats))
}

# Processing step.
extract_group_matrix <- function(df_raw, feature_cols, words) {
  idx <- match(words, df_raw$headword)
  ok  <- !is.na(idx)
  if (!all(ok)) {
    missing <- unique(words[!ok])
    if (length(missing) > 0) {
      warning("The following words were not found in the feature table and will be ignored:\n",
              paste(head(missing, 20), collapse = ", "),
              if (length(missing) > 20) " ...")
    }
  }
  idx_use <- idx[ok]
  if (length(idx_use) == 0) {
    return(list(words = character(0), mat = matrix(nrow = 0, ncol = length(feature_cols))))
  }
  sub <- df_raw[idx_use, feature_cols, drop = FALSE]
  m   <- as.matrix(sub)
  rownames(m) <- df_raw$headword[idx_use]
  list(words = df_raw$headword[idx_use], mat = m)
}

#####################
## Welch t-testï¼ˆindependentï¼‰
#####################

ttest_featurewise_welch <- function(mat_x, mat_y, feat_names,
                                    label_x = "group_X", label_y = "group_Y") {
  stopifnot(ncol(mat_x) == length(feat_names),
            ncol(mat_y) == length(feat_names))

  res_list <- lapply(seq_along(feat_names), function(j) {
    feat <- feat_names[j]
    x <- mat_x[, j]
    y <- mat_y[, j]

    # Processing step.
    x2 <- x[is.finite(x)]
    y2 <- y[is.finite(y)]
    n_x <- length(x2)
    n_y <- length(y2)

    if (n_x < 2 || n_y < 2) {
      return(tibble(
        feature   = feat,
        n_x       = n_x,
        n_y       = n_y,
        mean_x    = if (length(x2)) mean(x2) else NA_real_,
        mean_y    = if (length(y2)) mean(y2) else NA_real_,
        diff_mean = if (length(x2) && length(y2)) mean(x2) - mean(y2) else NA_real_,
        t_stat    = NA_real_,
        df        = NA_real_,
        p_value   = NA_real_,
        group_x   = label_x,
        group_y   = label_y
      ))
    }

    tt <- tryCatch(
      t.test(x2, y2, var.equal = FALSE),  # Welch independent t-test
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(tt)) {
      tibble(
        feature   = feat,
        n_x       = n_x,
        n_y       = n_y,
        mean_x    = mean(x2),
        mean_y    = mean(y2),
        diff_mean = mean(x2) - mean(y2),
        t_stat    = NA_real_,
        df        = NA_real_,
        p_value   = NA_real_,
        group_x   = label_x,
        group_y   = label_y
      )
    } else {
      tibble(
        feature   = feat,
        n_x       = n_x,
        n_y       = n_y,
        mean_x    = mean(x2),
        mean_y    = mean(y2),
        diff_mean = mean(x2) - mean(y2),
        t_stat    = unname(tt$statistic),
        df        = unname(tt$parameter),
        p_value   = tt$p.value,
        group_x   = label_x,
        group_y   = label_y
      )
    }
  })

  bind_rows(res_list) %>%
    mutate(
      mean_x    = round5(mean_x),
      mean_y    = round5(mean_y),
      diff_mean = round5(diff_mean),
      t_stat    = round5(t_stat),
      df        = round5(df),
      p_value   = round5(p_value)
    )
}

add_fdr_welch <- function(res_tbl, fdr_method = "BH") {
  if (all(is.na(res_tbl$p_value))) {
    res_tbl$p_adj <- NA_real_
  } else {
    res_tbl$p_adj <- p.adjust(res_tbl$p_value, method = fdr_method)
  }
  res_tbl %>%
    mutate(
      p_adj        = round5(p_adj),
      sig_FDR_0_05 = !is.na(p_adj) & p_adj < 0.05
    ) %>%
    select(feature, group_x, group_y,
           n_x, n_y,
           mean_x, mean_y, diff_mean,
           t_stat, df, p_value, p_adj,
           sig_FDR_0_05)
}

#####################
## Wilcoxon rank-sumï¼ˆindependentï¼‰
#####################

wilcox_featurewise_indep <- function(mat_x, mat_y, feat_names,
                                     label_x = "group_X", label_y = "group_Y") {
  stopifnot(ncol(mat_x) == length(feat_names),
            ncol(mat_y) == length(feat_names))

  res_list <- lapply(seq_along(feat_names), function(j) {
    feat <- feat_names[j]
    x <- mat_x[, j]
    y <- mat_y[, j]

    # Processing step.
    x2 <- x[is.finite(x)]
    y2 <- y[is.finite(y)]
    n_x <- length(x2)
    n_y <- length(y2)

    if (n_x < 1 || n_y < 1) {
      return(tibble(
        feature   = feat,
        n_x       = n_x,
        n_y       = n_y,
        W         = NA_real_,
        p_value   = NA_real_,
        median_x  = if (length(x2)) median(x2) else NA_real_,
        median_y  = if (length(y2)) median(y2) else NA_real_,
        group_x   = label_x,
        group_y   = label_y
      ))
    }

    wt <- tryCatch(
      wilcox.test(x2, y2, paired = FALSE, exact = FALSE),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(wt)) {
      tibble(
        feature   = feat,
        n_x       = n_x,
        n_y       = n_y,
        W         = NA_real_,
        p_value   = NA_real_,
        median_x  = median(x2),
        median_y  = median(y2),
        group_x   = label_x,
        group_y   = label_y
      )
    } else {
      tibble(
        feature   = feat,
        n_x       = n_x,
        n_y       = n_y,
        W         = unname(wt$statistic),
        p_value   = wt$p.value,
        median_x  = median(x2),
        median_y  = median(y2),
        group_x   = label_x,
        group_y   = label_y
      )
    }
  })

  bind_rows(res_list) %>%
    mutate(
      median_x = round5(median_x),
      median_y = round5(median_y),
      W        = round5(W),
      p_value  = round5(p_value)
    )
}

add_fdr_wilcox <- function(res_tbl, fdr_method = "BH") {
  if (all(is.na(res_tbl$p_value))) {
    res_tbl$p_adj <- NA_real_
  } else {
    res_tbl$p_adj <- p.adjust(res_tbl$p_value, method = fdr_method)
  }
  res_tbl %>%
    mutate(
      p_adj       = round5(p_adj),
      diff_median = round5(median_x - median_y),
      sig_FDR_0_05 = !is.na(p_adj) & p_adj < 0.05
    ) %>%
    select(feature, group_x, group_y,
           n_x, n_y,
           W, p_value, p_adj,
           median_x, median_y, diff_median,
           sig_FDR_0_05)
}

#####################
# Processing step.
#####################

message(">>> Reading summary file ...")
poly_sheet <- read_xlsx(SUMMARY_XLSX, sheet = "homonym_vs_polyseme")
mono_sheet <- read_xlsx(SUMMARY_XLSX, sheet = "homonym_vs_monoseme")

message(">>> Reading raw feature files ...")
hom_raw_info  <- read_raw_dedup(RAW_HOM,  head_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)
poly_raw_info <- read_raw_dedup(RAW_POLY, head_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)
mono_raw_info <- read_raw_dedup(RAW_MONO, head_col = COL_HEADWORD, h_col_index = H_COL_INDEX)

hom_df  <- hom_raw_info$df
poly_df <- poly_raw_info$df
mono_df <- mono_raw_info$df

hom_feats  <- hom_raw_info$feature_cols
poly_feats <- poly_raw_info$feature_cols
mono_feats <- mono_raw_info$feature_cols

common_feats <- get_common_features(hom_feats, poly_feats, mono_feats)
if (length(common_feats) == 0) {
  stop("The three raw feature tables have no common feature columns; check H_COL_INDEX or column names.")
} else {
  message("Number of common features: ", length(common_feats))
}

## ---------- Step1: hom vs poly ----------
message("Preparing Step1: homonym vs polyseme_candidate_instead_whole ...")

if (!("polyseme_candidate_instead_whole" %in% names(poly_sheet))) {
  stop("poly_sheet does not contain the polyseme_candidate_instead_whole column.")
}

pairs_poly <- poly_sheet %>%
  select(homonym, polyseme_candidate_instead_whole) %>%
  filter(!is.na(homonym),
         !is.na(polyseme_candidate_instead_whole))

hom_idx1  <- match(pairs_poly$homonym, hom_df$headword)
poly_idx1 <- match(pairs_poly$polyseme_candidate_instead_whole, poly_df$headword)
ok_pair1  <- !is.na(hom_idx1) & !is.na(poly_idx1)

hom_mat_1  <- as.matrix(hom_df[hom_idx1[ok_pair1],  common_feats, drop = FALSE])
poly_mat_1 <- as.matrix(poly_df[poly_idx1[ok_pair1], common_feats, drop = FALSE])

n_hom_step1  <- nrow(hom_mat_1)
n_poly_step1 <- nrow(poly_mat_1)

## ---------- Step2: hom vs ALL monoseme ----------
message("Preparing Step2: homonym vs all monoseme candidates ...")

cols_needed_mono <- c("monoseme_candidate", "alt1_word_all", "alt2_word_other")
missing_cols_mono <- setdiff(cols_needed_mono, names(mono_sheet))
if (length(missing_cols_mono) > 0) {
  stop("mono_sheet is missing columns: ", paste(missing_cols_mono, collapse = ", "))
}

hom_words_2 <- unique(na.omit(mono_sheet$homonym))  # Processing step.
mono_words_all <- c(
  mono_sheet$monoseme_candidate,
  mono_sheet$alt1_word_all,
  mono_sheet$alt2_word_other
)
mono_words_all <- unique(na.omit(mono_words_all))  # Processing step.

hom_group2  <- extract_group_matrix(hom_df,  common_feats, hom_words_2)
mono_group2 <- extract_group_matrix(mono_df, common_feats, mono_words_all)

hom_mat_2  <- hom_group2$mat
mono_mat_2 <- mono_group2$mat

n_hom_step2  <- nrow(hom_mat_2)
n_mono_step2 <- nrow(mono_mat_2)

## ---------- Step3: ALL poly vs ALL monoseme ----------
message("Preparing Step3: all polyseme candidates vs all monoseme candidates ...")

poly_words_all <- unique(na.omit(poly_sheet$polyseme_candidate_instead_whole))
poly_group3 <- extract_group_matrix(poly_df, common_feats, poly_words_all)
mono_group3 <- extract_group_matrix(mono_df, common_feats, mono_words_all)

poly_mat_3 <- poly_group3$mat
mono_mat_3 <- mono_group3$mat

n_poly_step3 <- nrow(poly_mat_3)
n_mono_step3 <- nrow(mono_mat_3)

## ---------- Step4: hom vs monoseme_candidate top1 ----------
message("Preparing Step4: homonym vs monoseme_candidate (top1) ...")

pairs_mono_top1 <- mono_sheet %>%
  select(homonym, monoseme_candidate) %>%
  filter(!is.na(homonym),
         !is.na(monoseme_candidate))

hom_idx4  <- match(pairs_mono_top1$homonym, hom_df$headword)
mono_idx4 <- match(pairs_mono_top1$monoseme_candidate, mono_df$headword)
ok_pair4  <- !is.na(hom_idx4) & !is.na(mono_idx4)

hom_mat_4  <- as.matrix(hom_df[hom_idx4[ok_pair4],  common_feats, drop = FALSE])
mono_mat_4 <- as.matrix(mono_df[mono_idx4[ok_pair4], common_feats, drop = FALSE])

n_hom_step4  <- nrow(hom_mat_4)
n_mono_step4 <- nrow(mono_mat_4)

## ---------- Step5: polyseme_candidate_instead_whole vs monoseme_candidate top1 ----------
message("Preparing Step5: polyseme_candidate_instead_whole vs monoseme_candidate (top1) ...")

poly_top <- poly_sheet %>%
  select(homonym, polyseme_candidate_instead_whole) %>%
  filter(!is.na(homonym),
         !is.na(polyseme_candidate_instead_whole))

mono_top <- mono_sheet %>%
  select(homonym, monoseme_candidate) %>%
  filter(!is.na(homonym),
         !is.na(monoseme_candidate))

pairs_poly_mono_top1 <- poly_top %>%
  inner_join(mono_top, by = "homonym") %>%
  distinct(homonym, polyseme_candidate_instead_whole, monoseme_candidate)

poly_idx5 <- match(pairs_poly_mono_top1$polyseme_candidate_instead_whole, poly_df$headword)
mono_idx5 <- match(pairs_poly_mono_top1$monoseme_candidate, mono_df$headword)
ok_pair5  <- !is.na(poly_idx5) & !is.na(mono_idx5)

poly_mat_5 <- as.matrix(poly_df[poly_idx5[ok_pair5], common_feats, drop = FALSE])
mono_mat_5 <- as.matrix(mono_df[mono_idx5[ok_pair5], common_feats, drop = FALSE])

n_poly_step5 <- nrow(poly_mat_5)
n_mono_step5 <- nrow(mono_mat_5)

############################################################
## Part 1: Welch's independent t-tests
############################################################
message(">>> Part 1: Welch independent t-tests ...")

welch_step1_raw <- ttest_featurewise_welch(
  mat_x = hom_mat_1,
  mat_y = poly_mat_1,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "homonym",
  label_y = "polyseme_candidate_instead_whole"
)
welch_step1 <- add_fdr_welch(welch_step1_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

welch_step2_raw <- ttest_featurewise_welch(
  mat_x = hom_mat_2,
  mat_y = mono_mat_2,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "homonym",
  label_y = "all_monoseme_candidates"
)
welch_step2 <- add_fdr_welch(welch_step2_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

welch_step3_raw <- ttest_featurewise_welch(
  mat_x = poly_mat_3,
  mat_y = mono_mat_3,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "all_polyseme_candidates",
  label_y = "all_monoseme_candidates"
)
welch_step3 <- add_fdr_welch(welch_step3_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

welch_step4_raw <- ttest_featurewise_welch(
  mat_x = hom_mat_4,
  mat_y = mono_mat_4,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "homonym",
  label_y = "monoseme_candidate_top1"
)
welch_step4 <- add_fdr_welch(welch_step4_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

welch_step5_raw <- ttest_featurewise_welch(
  mat_x = poly_mat_5,
  mat_y = mono_mat_5,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "polyseme_candidate_instead_whole",
  label_y = "monoseme_candidate_top1"
)
welch_step5 <- add_fdr_welch(welch_step5_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

############################################################
# Processing step.
############################################################
message(">>> Writing Welch t-test results ...")

wb_welch <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb_welch, "settings")
addWorksheet(wb_welch, "step1_hom_vs_poly")
addWorksheet(wb_welch, "step2_hom_vs_mono_all")
addWorksheet(wb_welch, "step3_poly_vs_mono_all")
addWorksheet(wb_welch, "step4_hom_vs_mono_top1")
addWorksheet(wb_welch, "step5_poly_vs_mono_top1")

writeData(
  wb_welch, "settings",
  tibble(
    note = c(
      "All tests are Welch's independent t-tests (two-sample).",
      "Step1: homonym vs polyseme_candidate_instead_whole.",
      "Step2: homonym vs ALL monoseme candidates (monoseme_candidate + alt1_word_all + alt2_word_other).",
      "Step3: ALL polyseme candidates vs ALL monoseme candidates.",
      "Step4: homonym vs monoseme_candidate (top1).",
      "Step5: polyseme_candidate_instead_whole vs monoseme_candidate (top1)."
    ),
    summary_file = SUMMARY_XLSX,
    raw_hom = RAW_HOM,
    raw_poly = RAW_POLY,
    raw_mono = RAW_MONO,
    H_COL_INDEX = H_COL_INDEX,
    common_feature_count = length(common_feats),
    n_hom_step1  = n_hom_step1,
    n_poly_step1 = n_poly_step1,
    n_hom_step2  = n_hom_step2,
    n_mono_step2 = n_mono_step2,
    n_poly_step3 = n_poly_step3,
    n_mono_step3 = n_mono_step3,
    n_hom_step4  = n_hom_step4,
    n_mono_step4 = n_mono_step4,
    n_poly_step5 = n_poly_step5,
    n_mono_step5 = n_mono_step5
  )
)

writeData(wb_welch, "step1_hom_vs_poly",       welch_step1)
writeData(wb_welch, "step2_hom_vs_mono_all",  welch_step2)
writeData(wb_welch, "step3_poly_vs_mono_all", welch_step3)
writeData(wb_welch, "step4_hom_vs_mono_top1", welch_step4)
writeData(wb_welch, "step5_poly_vs_mono_top1", welch_step5)

saveWorkbook(wb_welch, OUT_WELCH, overwrite = TRUE)
message("Welch t-test output written: ", OUT_WELCH)

############################################################
## Part 2: Wilcoxon rank-sum tests (independent)
############################################################
message(">>> Part 2: Wilcoxon rank-sum tests (independent) ...")

wilcox_step1_raw <- wilcox_featurewise_indep(
  mat_x = hom_mat_1,
  mat_y = poly_mat_1,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "homonym",
  label_y = "polyseme_candidate_instead_whole"
)
wilcox_step1 <- add_fdr_wilcox(wilcox_step1_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

wilcox_step2_raw <- wilcox_featurewise_indep(
  mat_x = hom_mat_2,
  mat_y = mono_mat_2,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "homonym",
  label_y = "all_monoseme_candidates"
)
wilcox_step2 <- add_fdr_wilcox(wilcox_step2_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

wilcox_step3_raw <- wilcox_featurewise_indep(
  mat_x = poly_mat_3,
  mat_y = mono_mat_3,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "all_polyseme_candidates",
  label_y = "all_monoseme_candidates"
)
wilcox_step3 <- add_fdr_wilcox(wilcox_step3_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

wilcox_step4_raw <- wilcox_featurewise_indep(
  mat_x = hom_mat_4,
  mat_y = mono_mat_4,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "homonym",
  label_y = "monoseme_candidate_top1"
)
wilcox_step4 <- add_fdr_wilcox(wilcox_step4_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

wilcox_step5_raw <- wilcox_featurewise_indep(
  mat_x = poly_mat_5,
  mat_y = mono_mat_5,
  feat_names = common_feats,
  label_x = "polyseme_candidate_instead_whole",
  label_y = "monoseme_candidate_top1"
)
wilcox_step5 <- add_fdr_wilcox(wilcox_step5_raw, fdr_method = FDR_METHOD)

############################################################
# Statistical testing step.
############################################################
message(">>> Writing Wilcoxon rank-sum results ...")

wb_wilcox <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb_wilcox, "settings")
addWorksheet(wb_wilcox, "step1_hom_vs_poly")
addWorksheet(wb_wilcox, "step2_hom_vs_mono_all")
addWorksheet(wb_wilcox, "step3_poly_vs_mono_all")
addWorksheet(wb_wilcox, "step4_hom_vs_mono_top1")
addWorksheet(wb_wilcox, "step5_poly_vs_mono_top1")

writeData(
  wb_wilcox, "settings",
  tibble(
    note = c(
      "All tests are Wilcoxon rank-sum tests (independent, non-parametric).",
      "Step1: homonym vs polyseme_candidate_instead_whole.",
      "Step2: homonym vs ALL monoseme candidates (monoseme_candidate + alt1_word_all + alt2_word_other).",
      "Step3: ALL polyseme candidates vs ALL monoseme candidates.",
      "Step4: homonym vs monoseme_candidate (top1).",
      "Step5: polyseme_candidate_instead_whole vs monoseme_candidate (top1)."
    ),
    summary_file = SUMMARY_XLSX,
    raw_hom = RAW_HOM,
    raw_poly = RAW_POLY,
    raw_mono = RAW_MONO,
    H_COL_INDEX = H_COL_INDEX,
    common_feature_count = length(common_feats),
    n_hom_step1  = n_hom_step1,
    n_poly_step1 = n_poly_step1,
    n_hom_step2  = n_hom_step2,
    n_mono_step2 = n_mono_step2,
    n_poly_step3 = n_poly_step3,
    n_mono_step3 = n_mono_step3,
    n_hom_step4  = n_hom_step4,
    n_mono_step4 = n_mono_step4,
    n_poly_step5 = n_poly_step5,
    n_mono_step5 = n_mono_step5
  )
)

writeData(wb_wilcox, "step1_hom_vs_poly",       wilcox_step1)
writeData(wb_wilcox, "step2_hom_vs_mono_all",  wilcox_step2)
writeData(wb_wilcox, "step3_poly_vs_mono_all", wilcox_step3)
writeData(wb_wilcox, "step4_hom_vs_mono_top1", wilcox_step4)
writeData(wb_wilcox, "step5_poly_vs_mono_top1", wilcox_step5)

saveWorkbook(wb_wilcox, OUT_WILCOX, overwrite = TRUE)
message("Wilcoxon rank-sum output written: ", OUT_WILCOX)
```

Expected output:
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/07_final_ttest/final_Welch_candidate_words_ttest.xlsx`
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/07_final_ttest/final_Wilcoxon_candidate_words_ttest.xlsx`

## Step 08: Export Final Selected Word Tables

Use the final sense-balanced summary to extract the selected homonym, polyseme, and monoseme rows from the cleaned lexical tables and write the final selected-word workbook.

```{r step_08}
# ====================== Configuration: change this to your path ======================
SUMMARY_XLSX <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/06_sense_counts/sense_counts_summary_WITH_poly3_mono_backups.xlsx"

HOM_EXAMPLE_FILE  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/homonym_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"
POLY_EXAMPLE_FILE <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/polyseme_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"
MONO_EXAMPLE_FILE <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/01_organization/monoseme_pos_sense_example_cleaned.xlsx"

OUT_DIR  <- "CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/08_final_all_words_select"
OUT_XLSX <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "final_all_words_selected.xlsx")

dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# ====================== Packages ======================
library(readxl)
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)

# ====================== Helper functions ======================
read_first_sheet <- function(path) {
  read_xlsx(path, sheet = 1)
}

get_col_loose <- function(df, target) {
  nm <- names(df)
  hit <- nm[trimws(tolower(nm)) == tolower(target)]
  if (length(hit) == 0) stop("Could not find required column: ", target)
  hit[1]
}

extract_rows_by_words <- function(example_file, words, keep_cols = NULL) {
  df <- read_first_sheet(example_file)
  head_col <- get_col_loose(df, "headword")
  words <- unique(na.omit(as.character(words)))
  out <- df %>% filter(.data[[head_col]] %in% words)

  if (!is.null(keep_cols)) {
    cols <- keep_cols[keep_cols %in% names(out)]
    out <- out[, cols, drop = FALSE]
  }
  out
}

# ====================== Read final candidate summary ======================
poly_sheet <- read_xlsx(SUMMARY_XLSX, sheet = "homonym_vs_polyseme")
mono_sheet <- read_xlsx(SUMMARY_XLSX, sheet = "homonym_vs_monoseme")

# Homonym words come from the final summary table.
hom_words <- unique(na.omit(poly_sheet$homonym))

# Final polyseme words use the sense-balanced replacement column.
if (!("polyseme_candidate_instead_whole" %in% names(poly_sheet))) {
  stop("poly_sheet does not contain polyseme_candidate_instead_whole.")
}
poly_words <- unique(na.omit(poly_sheet$polyseme_candidate_instead_whole))

# Final monoseme words combine the original candidate, alt1, and selected alt2 backup words.
mono_cols <- c("monoseme_candidate", "alt1_word_all", "alt2_word_other")
missing_mono_cols <- setdiff(mono_cols, names(mono_sheet))
if (length(missing_mono_cols) > 0) {
  stop("mono_sheet is missing required final-selection columns: ", paste(missing_mono_cols, collapse = ", "))
}
mono_words <- unique(na.omit(unlist(mono_sheet[mono_cols], use.names = FALSE)))

# ====================== Extract final rows from cleaned lexical tables ======================
final_homo <- extract_rows_by_words(
  HOM_EXAMPLE_FILE,
  hom_words,
  keep_cols = c("headword", "definition", "clean1", "pos", "sense1", "example1", "select", "homograph", "polyseme")
)

final_poly <- extract_rows_by_words(
  POLY_EXAMPLE_FILE,
  poly_words,
  keep_cols = c("headword", "definition", "clean1", "pos", "sense1", "example1")
)

final_mono <- extract_rows_by_words(
  MONO_EXAMPLE_FILE,
  mono_words,
  keep_cols = c("headword", "definition", "clean1", "pos", "sense1", "example1")
)

# ====================== Write final workbook ======================
wb <- createWorkbook()
addWorksheet(wb, "final_homo")
addWorksheet(wb, "final_poly")
addWorksheet(wb, "final_mono")
addWorksheet(wb, "settings")

writeData(wb, "final_homo", final_homo)
writeData(wb, "final_poly", final_poly)
writeData(wb, "final_mono", final_mono)

writeData(wb, "settings", tibble(
  note = c(
    "final_homo: homonym rows with definition/clean1/pos/sense1/example1 from the cleaned homonym table.",
    "final_poly: polyseme rows selected by polyseme_candidate_instead_whole from the cleaned polyseme table.",
    "final_mono: monoseme rows selected from monoseme_candidate + alt1_word_all + alt2_word_other from the cleaned monoseme table."
  ),
  summary_file = SUMMARY_XLSX,
  hom_example_file = HOM_EXAMPLE_FILE,
  poly_example_file = POLY_EXAMPLE_FILE,
  mono_example_file = MONO_EXAMPLE_FILE,
  n_final_homo_words = length(hom_words),
  n_final_poly_words = length(poly_words),
  n_final_mono_words = length(mono_words)
))

saveWorkbook(wb, OUT_XLSX, overwrite = TRUE)
message("Final selected-word workbook written: ", OUT_XLSX)
```

Expected output:
- `CHANGE_THIS_TO_YOUR_PATH/08_final_all_words_select/final_all_words_selected.xlsx`
